sections.content:
Interesting! After pressing SPACE, the character has changed appearance! Looking at the image:
- The character is now mostly green (N) instead of white (W)
- The red blocks have turned gray (g)
- The character's shape seems to have changed
This suggests that SPACE is a "transform" or "merge" action. The character has merged with the green frame, changing its color to green. The red blocks have also changed to gray.
Let me check the current state more carefully to understand what happened.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model updated: SPACE action transformed the character! The white character turned green and red blocks turned gray. This is a key mechanic - SPACE merges/transforms the character with the green frame.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Nodes after SPACE:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] > 5:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)} cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check ASCII
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCharacter area (rows 29-50, cols 36-54):")
for i in range(29, 51):
    print(f"  {i}: {ascii_lines[i][36:54]}")

print(f"\nLevel completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
